001package edu.boisestate.lowry.crypto;
002
003import java.nio.ByteBuffer;
004import java.security.SecureRandom;
005
006/**
007 * An implementation of the Counter (CTR) mode of operation.
008 *
009 * @author Jayce Lowry.
010 */
011public class CTRMode implements SymmetricCipher {
012    /**
013     * The underlying block cipher used.
014     */
015    private final BlockCipher blockCipher;
016    /**
017     * The underlying block cipher's block size.
018     */
019    private final int blockSize;
020
021    /**
022     * Creates a CTRMode instance with the given block
023     * cipher as the underlying cipher.
024     *
025     * @param cipher The underlying block cipher for this instance.
026     */
027    public CTRMode(BlockCipher cipher) {
028        this.blockCipher = cipher;
029        blockSize = cipher.getBlockSize();
030    }
031
032    @Override
033    public byte[] encrypt(byte[] plaintext, byte[] key) {
034        // Randomly generate a nonce
035        SecureRandom rand = new SecureRandom();
036        byte[] nonce = new byte[blockSize / 2];
037        rand.nextBytes(nonce);
038        // Prepare the counter with the nonce as the first half
039        byte[] counter = new byte[blockSize];
040        System.arraycopy(nonce, 0, counter, 0, nonce.length);
041        // Encrypt
042        byte[] ciphertext = runCTR(plaintext, key, counter);
043        // Prepend nonce to the ciphertext
044        ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(nonce.length + ciphertext.length);
045        buffer.put(nonce).put(ciphertext);
046        return buffer.array();
047    }
048
049    @Override
050    public byte[] decrypt(byte[] ciphertext, byte[] key) {
051        // Separate nonce and data
052        ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.wrap(ciphertext);
053        byte[] nonce = new byte[blockSize / 2];
054        byte[] encryptedData = new byte[ciphertext.length - (blockSize / 2)];
055        buffer.get(nonce).get(encryptedData);
056        // Prepare counter
057        byte[] counter = new byte[blockSize];
058        System.arraycopy(nonce, 0, counter, 0, nonce.length);
059        // Decrypt
060        return runCTR(encryptedData, key, counter);
061    }
062
063    /**
064     * Runs the CTR operation on the given text, using a secret key
065     * and initial counter.
066     *
067     * @param text The plaintext or ciphertext.
068     * @param key The secret key.
069     * @param counter The initial counter.
070     * @return The resulting processed text.
071     */
072    private byte[] runCTR(byte[] text, byte[] key, byte[] counter) {
073        // Copy data into a working output array
074        byte[] out = new byte[text.length];
075        System.arraycopy(text, 0, out, 0, text.length);
076
077        // XOR text data with the encrypted counter. Once each position
078        // of the current counter has been seen, re-encrypt it and increment.
079        byte[] encryptedCounter = new byte[0];
080        int j = blockSize;
081        for (int i = 0; i < text.length; i++) {
082            if (j == blockSize) {
083                encryptedCounter = blockCipher.encipher(counter, key);
084                incrementBlock(counter, blockSize / 2, counter.length);
085                j = 0;
086            }
087            out[i] ^= encryptedCounter[j];
088            j++;
089        }
090        return out;
091    }
092
093    /**
094     * Increments a specific sub-block of the given bytes, treating it as an
095     * unsigned integer (big endian). This is essentially a ripple-carry addition
096     * of the sub-block and 1.
097     *
098     * @param input The input block containing the sub-block to increment.
099     * @param start The index of the most significant byte of the sub-block, inclusive.
100     * @param end The index of the least significant byte of the sub-block, exclusive.
101     * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the input is null, the indices are out of bounds,
102     * or the ending index is before the starting index.
103     * @throws IllegalStateException If the entire block overflows.
104     */
105    protected void incrementBlock(byte[] input, int start, int end) {
106        if (input == null || start < 0 || end > input.length || end < start) {
107            throw new IllegalArgumentException();
108        } else if (start == end) {
109            return;
110        }
111        int i;
112        for (i = end - 1; i >= start; i--) {
113            input[i]++;
114            if (input[i] != 0) { // No carry
115                break;
116            }
117            // Carry
118        }
119        if (i < start) { // Overflow
120            throw new IllegalStateException();
121        }
122    }
123}
